summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity to third-party endpoint at install time
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests developer identity from the installer's machine: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI configuration file, which typically contains an oauth_token plus the authenticated GitHub username/email), and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), os.platform(), and CI provider information. The collected identity object (hostname, username, scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, cwd) is POSTed via https.request to a hardcoded Google Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a destination unrelated to OpenAI or to the package's stated homepage (openai-agents-js.guide). The script frames the behavior as 'anonymous diagnostics,' but hostname, OS username, SCM email, GitHub login, and working directory are direct PII / developer identifiers, not anonymous metrics. The package name and author string impersonate the OpenAI Agents SDK ecosystem (the legitimate package is @openai/agents, which is also declared as a dependency to make this lure functional), trading on the OpenAI brand to attract developers building agent applications.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
